entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Legendary reliability; the workhorse diesel that defined early Caddy durability with pump‑diesel injection.

commonFailures: Injector failure at 80k-120k miles (most common replacement trigger)
DPF regeneration issues on urban use

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Popular petrol option for Mk4 Caddy; good balance of power and economy but timing chain concerns.

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol

commonProblems.h2: Volkswagen Caddy Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volkswagen Caddy's diesel engines (1.6 TDI CAYC, 2.0 TDI CFHC/CUUD) dominate UK replacement enquiries due to injector and oil pump failures, while petrol 1.2/1.4 TSI units (CHGA, CMAB) suffer timing chain issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare realistic repair vs replacement costs.

problems.h4: Caddy 1.6 TDI Injector Failure - Why CAYC Engines Need Replacement
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Caddy Startline, Trendline, C20, Life (2010-2020, CAYC & CLCA 1.6 TDI)
problems.rootCause: High‑pressure common‑rail injectors wear internally, causing misfires, rough idle and excessive fuel consumption; replacement of all four is often uneconomical vs engine swap.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; other injectors likely to follow within 12 months

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors, fuel pressure test, ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy and under 100k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when van worth £3k+; eliminates injector risk entirely

problems.recommendation: On a Caddy valued over £3,000 with injector failure beyond 90k miles, a rebuilt CAYC engine with upgraded injectors offers better long‑term value than repeated injector repairs. Budget £1,700-£2,500 all‑in for supply & fit via our network.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CAYC engine prices for your Volkswagen Caddy

problems.h4: Caddy 2.0 TDI Oil Pump Failure - The CFHC/CUUD Catastrophe
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Caddy C20, Highline, Maxi, 4Motion (2010-Present, CFHC & CUUD 2.0 TDI)
problems.rootCause: Oil pump drive shaft wears or snaps, causing sudden loss of oil pressure; engine seizes within minutes if driven after warning lights appear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump drive shaft, new gaskets, oil change
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable BEFORE failure; requires early diagnosis via oil pressure test

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded oil pump drive, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only realistic option after seizure; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: If your Caddy has lost oil pressure or seized, replacement is the only option. For preventative peace of mind on high‑mileage 2.0 TDI Caddies, budget £900 for an oil pump drive upgrade before 100k miles.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CFHC/CUUD Volkswagen Caddy engine replacement

problems.h4: Caddy 1.2/1.4 TSI Timing Chain Failure - Petrol Engine Risk
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Caddy Trendline, Life, Highline (2010-2020, CHGA 1.2 TSI & CMAB 1.4 TSI)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wears, allowing chain stretch; if ignored, chain skips teeth causing valve/piston collision and catastrophic engine damage.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Chain Kit Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Highly recommended at 80k miles; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with revised tensioner design, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Required if chain has jumped; only option after valve damage

problems.recommendation: If you hear chain rattle on cold start, act immediately. A preventative chain kit replacement at £700-£950 is far cheaper than a seized engine. If damage has occurred, a rebuilt petrol engine is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CHGA/CMAB engine prices for your Volkswagen Caddy

problems.h4: Caddy DPF & EGR Blockage - Diesel Exhaust System Failures
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Caddy Panel Van, Maxi, Life (2010-Present, all 1.6/2.0 TDI diesel engines)
problems.rootCause: Short‑journey driving prevents DPF regeneration; soot buildup combined with EGR carbon deposits restricts airflow, triggering limp mode and eventual engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off‑vehicle DPF clean, EGR blanking plate fitted, ECU remap
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for urban vans; may affect MOT/emissions compliance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New/reconditioned DPF, EGR valve replacement, full system clean
repairOptions.longevity: Restores OEM spec; recommended if van kept long‑term

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR/DPF components, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when DPF/EGR damage has caused turbo or injector failure

problems.recommendation: For Caddies used primarily on short trips, preventative DPF cleaning every 30k miles and EGR blanking can extend engine life. If limp mode is persistent and diagnostics show turbo/injector damage, replacement is more economical than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Caddy diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Caddy Turbo Failure Symptoms - Loss of Power & Black Smoke
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Caddy all diesel variants (2004-Present, BJB, CAYC, CFHC, CUUD, CLCA)
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil seals wear or wastegate actuators stick, causing boost loss, black smoke, or oil consumption; often triggered by delayed oil changes or DPF issues.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace actuator or repair wastegate mechanism
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary if turbo internals are worn; diagnostic essential first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned turbo, new gaskets, oil feed flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy and under 120k miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new/upgraded turbo, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused oil starvation or engine damage

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure is isolated and caught early, a reconditioned turbo at £700-£1,300 is cost‑effective. If black smoke was accompanied by knocking or oil loss, the engine may already be damaged - replacement is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Compare Volkswagen Caddy turbo replacement engine prices

problems.h4: Caddy Crankshaft/Camshaft Sensor Faults - Intermittent Stalling
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Caddy all variants (2004-Present, all engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Sensors fail due to heat/vibration; causes intermittent stalling, no‑start, or cutting out when warm - often misdiagnosed as fuel or ignition issues.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty sensor, clear codes, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Highly effective if sensor is sole cause; cheap first diagnostic step

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Only required if sensor failure was symptom of deeper electrical/engine damage
repairOptions.longevity: Rarely needed for sensor faults alone; replacement only if misdiagnosis caused damage

problems.recommendation: Always start with a proper diagnostic scan (£50-£80) before assuming major engine work. Crank/cam sensor faults are common, cheap to fix, and rarely require engine replacement unless ignored for months.
problems.cta: Get a diagnostic quote for your Volkswagen Caddy before committing to engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volkswagen Caddy
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Caddy suffers injector failure, oil pump seizure, timing chain stretch or DPF blockage, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volkswagen Caddy specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volkswagen Caddy Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volkswagen Caddy and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Volkswagen Caddy Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Volkswagen Caddy is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Volkswagen Caddy 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6 TDI common‑rail diesel powered most Mk3 and early Mk4 Caddies, offering a balance of economy and torque for commercial use, though injector sensitivity became a key replacement driver.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration issues on urban use
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Caddy 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Caddy 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legendary 1.9 TDI PD engine defined early Caddy reliability with robust mechanical injection, though injector pump wear and turbo issues drive replacement on high‑mileage examples.
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Caddy 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Caddy 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0 TDI common‑rail became the performance diesel choice for Maxi, 4Motion and Highline Caddies, but oil pump drive failure remains a critical replacement trigger.
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump drive failure causing seizure
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Caddy 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Caddy 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The downsized 1.0 TSI EA211 turbo petrol arrived in Mk5 Caddies, delivering strong economy but requiring strict servicing to protect the timing belt-driven design.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Caddy 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Caddy 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2 TSI was an early petrol option for Mk3 Caddies but gained notoriety for timing chain tensioner failures that often led to catastrophic engine damage.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Caddy 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Caddy 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4 TSI offered a refined petrol alternative for Mk4 Caddies, though timing chain stretch and high‑pressure fuel pump wear remain common replacement drivers.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Caddy 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Caddy 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The latest 1.5 TSI evo with ACT cylinder deactivation debuted in Mk5 Caddies, offering efficiency gains but still accumulating early‑life reliability data.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Caddy 1.5L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Volkswagen Caddy Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Volkswagen Caddy engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.9 TDI workhorses to the latest 1.5 TSI evo units.

items.title: Volkswagen Caddy Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel engines dominate Caddy replacement enquiries due to high commercial mileage and known failure patterns in the 1.6/2.0 TDI common‑rail families.
items.descriptor: Diesel engines dominate Caddy replacement enquiries due to high commercial mileage and known failure patterns in the 1.6/2.0 TDI common‑rail families.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen Caddy Diesel Engine Families
families: CAYC - most searched replacement engine (1.6 TDI), injector failure at 80k-120k miles
CFHC/CUUD - 2.0 TDI oil pump drive failure risk, high torque for Maxi/4Motion
BJB/BKC - legendary 1.9 TDI reliability, injector pump wear on high mileage
foundIn: Mk3 Caddy (2004-2015) - BJB, BKC, CAYC, CFHC
knownFor: Strong torque for loaded commercial use
Injector failure (1.6 TDI) and oil pump failure (2.0 TDI) drive most replacements
DPF/EGR issues on urban‑use vans accelerate wear
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Caddy Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Volkswagen Caddy Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Caddies appeal to lower‑mileage or passenger‑focused owners, but timing chain issues in early TSI units create distinct replacement patterns.
items.descriptor: Petrol Caddies appeal to lower‑mileage or passenger‑focused owners, but timing chain issues in early TSI units create distinct replacement patterns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen Caddy Petrol Engine Families
families: CHGA - 1.2 TSI timing chain tensioner failure (catastrophic if ignored)
CMAB - 1.4 TSI chain stretch and HPFP wear at higher mileage
DFYB/DFSB - latest 1.0/1.5 TSI evo, carbon buildup on direct injection
foundIn: Mk4 Caddy (2015-2020) - CMAB 1.4 TSI, CPVA 1.4 TGI CNG
Mk5 Caddy (2020-Present) - DFYB 1.0 TSI, DFSB 1.5 TSI evo
knownFor: Quieter, smoother operation vs diesel
Timing chain failures (1.2/1.4 TSI) are the primary replacement driver
Lower torque but adequate for lighter commercial or passenger use
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Caddy Petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Caddy Alternative Fuel Engines
items.description: No factory hybrid, plug‑in hybrid or pure electric Caddy variants were sold in the UK; CNG (1.4 TGI) was offered briefly but is now rare in the replacement market.
items.descriptor: No factory hybrid, plug‑in hybrid or pure electric Caddy variants were sold in the UK; CNG (1.4 TGI) was offered briefly but is now rare in the replacement market.
families: Dual‑fuel system: petrol for starting, CNG for primary running
Separate CNG tank in underfloor location; reduced boot space
Lower fuel costs but limited refuelling infrastructure in UK
foundIn: Caddy Life TGI, Caddy Highline TGI (2015-2020)
importantNotes: CNG engine replacement requires specialist knowledge of dual‑fuel systems
Availability of rebuilt CPVA engines is limited; lead times may apply
Most owners opt for petrol conversion or standard petrol engine replacement

engineYears.h2: Volkswagen Caddy Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the Volkswagen Caddy's UK production history, helping you identify your engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The third‑generation Caddy launched with the robust 1.9 TDI PD diesel, establishing the model's commercial reputation.
keyChanges: New PQ35 platform shared with Golf Mk5
1.9 TDI PD (BJB/BKC) became the default commercial engine
Panel Van and passenger Life variants introduced
mainEngines: BJB - 1.9 diesel PD (Startline / C20 / Panel Van)
knownFor: Legendary 1.9 TDI reliability with simple mechanical injection
Injector pump wear on high‑mileage examples
Basic but durable interior suited to trades use
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2010 Volkswagen Caddy replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Caddy 1.9 TDI engine replacement • BJB injector pump repair cost • Caddy Panel Van engine swap

years.preview: Mk3 Facelift: Common‑Rail Diesel & TSI Petrol Arrive
years.description: The 2010 facelift introduced common‑rail 1.6/2.0 TDI diesels and the first TSI petrols, improving emissions but adding complexity.
keyChanges: 1.6 TDI (CAYC) and 2.0 TDI (CFHC) replaced 1.9 PD units
First TSI petrol engines: 1.2 TSI (CHGA) and 1.4 TSI (CMAB)
Start/Stop technology added to improve economy
knownFor: CAYC injector failure at 80k-120k miles drives most replacements
CHGA timing chain tensioner failures cause catastrophic damage
Improved refinement but higher repair complexity vs 1.9 PD
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2015 Volkswagen Caddy replacement engines
years.ticker: Caddy 1.6 TDI injector failure • Caddy 1.2 TSI timing chain replacement • CFHC oil pump failure

years.preview: Mk4 Generation: Euro 6, Updated TDI & TSI Families
years.description: The Mk4 Caddy adopted Euro 6 emissions standards, bringing AdBlue to 2.0 TDI units and revised EA211 petrol engines.
keyChanges: Euro 6 compliance: AdBlue/SCR added to 2.0 TDI (CUUD)
EA211 petrol family refined: 1.0 TSI (DFYB) and 1.4 TSI (CMAB) continued
knownFor: CUUD retains oil pump drive vulnerability despite Euro 6 updates
CLCA injector issues persist but with improved calibration
CMAB timing chain stretch remains a key failure point
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2020 Volkswagen Caddy replacement engines
years.ticker: Caddy Maxi engine replacement • CUUD oil pump upgrade • Caddy Alltrack 4Motion engine

years.preview: Mk5 Generation: MQB Platform, Latest TSI & TDI Evo
years.description: The all‑new MQB‑based Mk5 Caddy introduced the latest EA211 evo petrol and EA288 evo diesel engines with enhanced efficiency.
keyChanges: New MQB platform with updated chassis and electronics
1.5 TSI evo (DFSB) with ACT cylinder deactivation
2.0 TDI evo (DSZA) with improved SCR/AdBlue system
mainEngines: DSZA - 2.0 TDI evo 122-150hp (Maxi / Panel Van / Highline)
knownFor: Early reliability data still accumulating; carbon buildup on direct injection noted
DSZA benefits from revised oil pump design vs older CFHC/CUUD
Higher complexity increases specialist repair costs vs earlier generations
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2024 Volkswagen Caddy replacement engines
years.ticker: Caddy Mk5 engine replacement • 1.5 TSI evo carbon clean • DSZA AdBlue system fault

years.preview: Mk1/Mk2 Caddy: Classic Pickup & Van Engines
years.description: Early Caddy models shared Golf Mk1/Mk2 underpinnings, offering simple diesel and petrol units now sought by restorers.
keyChanges: Mk1 (1990-1996): Golf Mk1‑based pickup with 1.6D (CK) or 1.8 petrol (JH)
Mk2 (1996-2003): Golf Mk3‑based van with 1.9 SDI (AEY) or 1.4/1.6 petrol
knownFor: Extremely simple mechanics; easy DIY repair
Rust and corrosion are bigger concerns than engine failure
Rare engines command premium prices in the classic market
years.cta: Enquire about classic Volkswagen Caddy engine availability

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Volkswagen Caddy was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Volkswagen Caddy specialists.

faq.h2: Volkswagen Caddy Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Volkswagen Caddy engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Volkswagen Caddy engine prices typically range from £1,195 to £2,595 supply‑only, depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £400‑£800 labour.
keyPoints: 1.6 TDI CAYC rebuilt: £1,295‑£1,795 supply only
2.0 TDI CFHC/CUUD rebuilt: £1,495‑£2,295 supply only
Petrol 1.2/1.4 TSI rebuilt: £1,195‑£1,995 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically £400‑£800 nationwide
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Volkswagen Caddy engine prices for your variant

items.question: Which Volkswagen Caddy engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The 1.9 TDI PD (BJB/BKC) is widely regarded as the most reliable Volkswagen Caddy engine, with simple mechanical injection and proven longevity when serviced.
keyPoints: BJB/BKC 1.9 TDI: robust, easy to repair, high mileage potential
Avoid early 1.2 TSI (CHGA) due to timing chain tensioner risk
2.0 TDI (CFHC/CUUD) reliable if oil pump drive is preventative‑replaced
Regular oil changes critical for all Caddy diesel longevity
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable Volkswagen Caddy 1.9 TDI engine replacement

items.question: What causes Volkswagen Caddy 1.6 TDI injector failure?
items.answer: Volkswagen Caddy 1.6 TDI (CAYC) injector failure stems from high‑pressure common‑rail wear, causing misfires, rough idle and excessive fuel consumption at 80k‑120k miles.
keyPoints: CAYC injectors sensitive to fuel quality and servicing intervals
Symptoms: misfire codes, black smoke, hard starting when warm
Replacing all four injectors often costs more than a rebuilt engine
Upgraded injectors in rebuilt units address the root cause
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Caddy CAYC engine replacement quotes

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high‑mileage Volkswagen Caddy?
items.answer: Replacing the engine on a Volkswagen Caddy worth £3,000+ is usually worthwhile, especially with a rebuilt unit carrying a 12‑month warranty versus repeated repairs.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost £1,200‑£2,300 + £400‑£800 fit
Extends van life by 80k‑120k miles with warranty protection
Cheaper than dealer replacement; retains commercial utility
Consider van's body/chassis condition before committing
items.cta: Get a tailored Volkswagen Caddy engine replacement quote today

items.question: What is the most common Volkswagen Caddy engine problem?
items.answer: Injector failure on the 1.6 TDI (CAYC) and oil pump drive failure on the 2.0 TDI (CFHC/CUUD) are the two most common Volkswagen Caddy engine problems driving replacement enquiries.
keyPoints: CAYC injector failure: 80k‑120k miles, misfires, rough running
CFHC/CUUD oil pump failure: sudden seizure, loss of oil pressure
Petrol 1.2/1.4 TSI: timing chain stretch causing catastrophic damage
DPF/EGR blockage accelerates wear on urban‑use diesel Caddies
items.cta: Find Volkswagen Caddy engine specialists for your failure type

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Volkswagen Caddy?
items.answer: While technically possible, fitting a non‑original engine in a Volkswagen Caddy requires extensive modification, ECU reprogramming and may fail MOT emissions tests.
keyPoints: Stick to original engine codes for straightforward replacement
Swapping between CAYC/CLCA or CFHC/CUUD is feasible with coding
Non‑VW engines require custom mounts, wiring, exhaust work
Insurance and MOT implications make like‑for‑like replacement safest
items.cta: Get quotes for correct engine code Volkswagen Caddy replacement

items.question: How long should a Volkswagen Caddy engine last?
items.answer: With proper servicing, a Volkswagen Caddy diesel engine can last 150,000‑200,000 miles; petrol TSI units may reach 120,000‑150,000 miles if timing components are maintained.
keyPoints: 1.9 TDI (BJB): 200k+ miles achievable with oil changes every 10k
1.6/2.0 TDI: 150k‑180k miles if injectors/oil pump addressed
1.2/1.4 TSI: 120k‑150k miles with preventative timing chain service
Commercial use and short trips reduce all engine lifespans
items.cta: Compare long‑life rebuilt Volkswagen Caddy engine options

items.question: What is the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt Volkswagen Caddy engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Volkswagen Caddy engines are cleaned and fitted with new wear items; rebuilt units include upgraded components targeting known failure points like injectors or oil pumps.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: new gaskets, seals, bearings; tested pre‑dispatch
Rebuilt: adds upgraded injectors, revised oil pump drives, improved tensioners
Rebuilt typically costs £100‑£300 more but offers longer reliability
Both carry minimum 12‑month warranty via EngineMarket network
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt Volkswagen Caddy engine prices

items.question: Do I need to replace the DPF when replacing my Volkswagen Caddy engine?
items.answer: DPF replacement is not automatically required when replacing a Volkswagen Caddy engine, but inspection is recommended; a blocked DPF can damage a new engine.
keyPoints: Test DPF backpressure before fitting rebuilt engine
Cleaning costs £200‑£400; replacement £600‑£1,200
Urban‑use Caddies more likely to need DPF attention
Some specialists offer DPF‑delete options (check MOT/emissions compliance)
items.cta: Get a full Volkswagen Caddy engine + exhaust system quote

items.question: How quickly can I get a Volkswagen Caddy engine delivered?
items.answer: Most Volkswagen Caddy rebuilt engines are dispatched within 24‑48 hours from UK suppliers, with next‑day courier delivery available for urgent commercial breakdowns.
keyPoints: Standard delivery: 2‑3 working days nationwide
Next‑day courier available for urgent breakdowns (extra cost)
Supply & fit bookings can often be arranged within 3‑5 days
Enter your registration for fastest, accurate quote matching
items.cta: Get urgent Volkswagen Caddy engine delivery quotes now
